task_completion.description: The target color photograph is opened in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP interface is visible with a color photograph loaded in the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The image is desaturated to remove original colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Desaturate' dialog box is visible, or the image on the canvas has been visibly converted to grayscale/black and white.
The 'Colorize' dialog box is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Sepia tone settings are applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Colorize dialog, the Hue is set to a warm tone (around 20-50 degrees, or 0.05-0.14 if normalized) and Saturation is adjusted to around 20-50% (0.2-0.5). The image on the canvas visibly changes to a vintage brownish-yellow sepia tone.

task_completion.description: The modified image is saved or exported.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Image', 'Save', or 'Overwrite' dialog is visible and confirmed, or a terminal/file manager shows the updated file.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to edit the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks and interaction with GIMP menus and dialogs, rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The original image was actually modified.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression from color to grayscale to sepia is visible on the same base image, proving it wasn't just replaced with a pre-made sepia image.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripts to bypass manual GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: No Python or GIMP batch scripts are seen being executed in the terminal to perform the image manipulation automatically.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP's Desaturate and Colorize tools to convert a color photograph to sepia. A valid sepia tone has a Hue in the 20-50 degree range (or 0.05-0.14 if normalized 0.0-1.0) and Saturation in the 10-50% range (0.1-0.5). The final image must visibly show a warm brownish-yellow vintage tint.
